post.summary: Search assets that match a certain criteria

get.description:
List the values that are valid in the [`POST /assets/_search`](/reference/search_assets__search_post) endpoint's `field` parameter.

Includes:
* Common Armis fields.
* Custom fields (also reflected in the [`GET /settings/device-custom-properties`](/reference/get_settings_device_custom_properties_get#/) endpoint).
* Integration fields.

get.summary: Get the details required to download and run a collector's image
Get the URLs of the latest data exports of a particular entity.

post.summary: Create an access token for consuming the API.
post.description:
Use this endpoint to create an access token.

You then authorize calls to the other endpoints by adding the header: "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>".

get.summary: Get a device custom property configuration.

delete.summary: Delete a device custom property configuration.

patch.summary: Update a device custom property configuration.

get.summary: List all device custom property configurations.

post.summary: Create a new device custom property configuration.

audience.title: Audience
audience.description: Value has to be the full tenant's domain with scheme and trailing slash

client_id.title: Client Id
client_id.description: Use email of the user that created the 'client_secret'

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: To obtain the secret, go to https://<tenant>.armis.com/settings/api-management

vendor_id.title: Vendor Id
vendor_id.description: The Vendor ID you received upon singing up to the developer portal

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: A list of scopes to grant access to

asset_id.title: Asset Id
asset_id.description:
The identifier that will be used to find the asset that needs to be updated.

The content type will vary according to the selected `asset_id_source` field (e.g. IP v4, Asset ID, etc.).

Generally speaking, all identifiers except Asset ID are provided by the customer's environment.

key.title: Key
key.description:
The key of the asset you wish to update.
* Currently, only custom properties are supported.
* For custom properties, add the `custom.` prefix. (e.g. `custom.MY_CUSTOM_PROPERTY`).
* To see the available custom properties, please refer to the [List all device custom property configurations](/reference/get_settings_device_custom_properties_get#/) endpoint.

asset_id.title: Asset Id
asset_id.description:
The identifier that will be used to find the asset that needs to be updated.

The content type will vary according to the selected `asset_id_source` field (e.g. IP v4, Asset ID, etc.).

Generally speaking, all identifiers except Asset ID are provided by the customer's environment.

key.title: Key
key.description:
The key of the asset you wish to update.
* Currently, only custom properties are supported.
* For custom properties, add the `custom.` prefix. (e.g. `custom.MY_CUSTOM_PROPERTY`).
* To see the available custom properties, please refer to the [List all device custom property configurations](/reference/get_settings_device_custom_properties_get#/) endpoint.

asset_id_source.description:
Some assets may have multiple unique identifiers.

For example, a device can have an Asset ID, a MAC Address, an IPv4, etc.

This field determines which field will be used to find the asset.

image_password.title: Image Password
image_password.description: The password for the OS that is encapsulated by the image.

url.title: Url
url.description: The temporary, presigned URL from which the OS image file can be downloaded.

CollectorImageDetails.title: CollectorImageDetails
CollectorImageDetails.description: Detailed information required to securely download a collector's image.

examples.description: The year the device was built.
examples.name: BuildYear

CustomPropertyField-Input.title: CustomPropertyField
CustomPropertyField-Input.description:
This model represents a device custom property field.
It makes sure that it has a prefix "custom."

enum: Microsoft System Center Virtual Machine Manager
DataSource.title: DataSource

asset_id_source.description:
Some assets may have multiple unique identifiers.

For example, a device can have an Asset ID, a MAC Address, an IPv4, etc.

This field determines which field will be used to find the asset.

data_sources.title: Data Sources
data_sources.description: List of data sources to filter on

last_seen_ge.title: Last Seen Ge
last_seen_ge.description: Last seen after (or exactly on) this date (in UTC).

last_seen_seconds.title: Last Seen Seconds
last_seen_seconds.description: Last seen in the last X seconds.

IntegrationPropertyField-Input.title: IntegrationPropertyField
IntegrationPropertyField-Input.description:
This model represents a device integration property field.
It makes sure that it has a prefix "integration.<something>."

examples.description: The year the device was first seen.

items.description: The year the device was built.
items.name: BuildYear

location.title: Location
location.description: The name of the location of the site, such as an address. When this field is set, the `lat` and `lng` are automatically derived from it.
examples: 548 Market Street Suite 97439 San Francisco, CA 94104-5401

asq_rule.description: Devices matching this ASQ rule will be associated with this site.

network_equipment_device_ids.title: Network Equipment Device Ids
network_equipment_device_ids.description: Devices that are detected through these network equipment devices will be associated with this site.

integration_ids.title: Integration Ids
integration_ids.description: Devices that are detected through these integration will be associated with this site.

lat.title: Latitude
lat.description: The latitude coordinate of the physical location of the site on earth. This field is read-only and is automatically derived from the `location` field.

lng.title: Longitude
lng.description: The longitude coordinate of the physical location of the site on earth. This field is read-only and is automatically derived from the `location` field.

examples.location: 548 Market Street Suite 97439 San Francisco, CA 94104-5401
examples.name: San Francisco HQ

items.name: San Francisco HQ
